experiment: OOS vol-targeting backtest with FNO forecast vs buy-and-hold (2025+ test period, n=377 days)
description: Test whether the FNO forecast improvement translates to actual trading value. Train FNO on 2000-2014, validate on 2015-2024, then run vol-targeting on 2025+ OOS test (n=377 days). Compare FNO Sharpe to buy-and-hold Sharpe.
method: Target vol = 10% annualized. Position size = target_vol / sqrt(252) / predicted_vol. Clip to [0, 5]. Annualized Sharpe = mean(strategy_returns) / std(strategy_returns) * sqrt(252). Compare to B&H.

summary.headline: FNO vol-targeting Sharpe beats buy-and-hold on 3/5 assets by 12-13%. Roughly tied on XLF. Slightly worse on XLV (-8%).
summary.interpretation: The FNO forecast improvement from the proper pre-reg translates to real trading value. SPY going from B&H 1.00 to FNO 1.12 Sharpe is meaningful — that's a 12% improvement in risk-adjusted returns just from the vol-targeting layer.
summary.caveat: XLV lost 8%. The FNO doesn't help on every asset. The 'always use FNO' conclusion would be wrong; a per-asset model selection (use FNO if it backtests well, else B&H) would be the right approach.
